body_txt: When a show removes a perfectly innocuous word or words in a sentence, giving it dirty connotations. This can be done intentionally for comedy, to parody censorship, or unintentionally as a result of actual censorship. Intentionally bleeping out sections of innocent dialogue to make it sound dirty for comedic purposes has become known as Unnecessary Censorship. Depending on the medium, may be used in conjunction with Narrative Profanity Filter , Symbol Swearing , Fun with Subtitles , or Saying Sound Effects Out Loud (if someone says "bleep"). Compare and contrast Signs of Disrepair , Innocent Innuendo , Accidental Innuendo , Partially-Concealed-Label Gag . See also Cluster *Bleep*-Bomb , Sound-Effect Bleep , Manipulative Editing , Scunthorpe Problem , *Bleep*-dammit! , Smurfing , and T-Word Euphemism . If Played for Laughs , particularly if it makes the listener wonder just what was said, then it is often an instance of Nothing Is Funnier .
